utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. All 5 appear in the final plan. Check full specification: (1) health_coverage requires physical and mental health insurance with costs — plan specifies type, monthly cost, and mentions mental health and wellness; ok. (2) property_coverage requires home/contents insurance with costs — plan provides type, monthly cost, address, but does not explicitly mention contents; however it says 'Luxury property protection' which reasonably implies home coverage; still, 'home/contents' is requested, and contents is not explicitly stated. This risks being incomplete. (3) travel_coverage requires travel insurance with costs — provided type, monthly cost; ok. (4) additional_services requires any enhancement services with costs — it lists membership with one-time cost and discounts applied; ok. (5) total_budget requires complete monthly/annual cost breakdown — it lists monthly total, annual total, one-time fees, and savings; ok. Only potential gap: property_coverage lacks explicit contents coverage, which the format explicitly asks for 'home/contents insurance with costs'. Therefore, 4 fully specified out of 5.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists three specific policy types: PrimeCare Premium Plans with Enhanced Stress Management (health), EliteHome Premium Coverage (property), and EliteTravel Premium Plan (travel). I matched each to the closest exact names in the ground-truth ratings. For health, the ground-truth includes PrimeCare Premium Plan with ratings of 10 for both physical and mental health; the plan's label includes 'PrimeCare Premium' and stress management, so it aligns with PrimeCare Premium Plan. For property, EliteHome Premium Coverage exactly matches with rating 10. For travel, EliteTravel Premium Plan exactly matches with rating 10.
